title: Ensure that the --auto-tls argument is not set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the --auto-tls argument is not set to true
input_row.description: Do not use self-signed certificates for TLS.
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly-available key value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should not be available to unauthenticated clients. You should enable the client authentication via valid certificates to secure the access to the etcd service. Impact: Clients will not be able to use self-signed certificates for TLS.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the etcd server node: ps -ef | grep etcd Verify that if the --auto-tls argument exists, it is not set to true.
input_row.remediation: Edit the etcd pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml on the master node and either remove the --auto-tls parameter or set it to false. --auto-tls=false Default Value: By default, --auto-tls is set to false. References: 1. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/ 3. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/configuration.html#auto-tls
input_row.impact: Clients will not be able to use self-signed certificates for TLS.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_etcd_auto_tls_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the etcd pod specification running on the control plane node(s). This is typically managed as a static Pod manifest at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml`, which is loaded as a Pod in the `kube-system` namespace.
- Use the Kubernetes API or `kubectl` to retrieve the etcd Pod spec:
  - Command: `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=etcd -o json`
  - Alternatively, directly inspect the static Pod manifest if accessible via automation.
- Parse the `spec.containers[].command` or `spec.containers[].args` array for the presence of the `--auto-tls` flag.
- Pass Criteria:
  - The `--auto-tls` flag is either not present (default is false), or explicitly set to `false` (i.e., `--auto-tls=false`).
- Fail Criteria:
  - The `--auto-tls` flag is present and set to `true` (i.e., `--auto-tls=true`).
- Example automated logic:
  1. For each etcd Pod/container, extract the command/args.
  2. Search for any argument matching `--auto-tls`.
  3. If found, ensure it is not set to `true`.
  4. If not found, consider compliant (default is false).
- This check can be fully automated via Kubernetes API or `kubectl` without requiring host-level access.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11 PDF
